story.iWant: EndorService to accept dependencies via constructor injection
story.soThat: I can customize implementations and enable comprehensive testing
story.tasks: - Task 1: Design dependency injection constructor interface (AC: 1, 4) - Define NewEndorServiceWithDeps() signature with interface parameters - Add dependency validation with structured error messages - Update EndorService struct to hold interface fields instead of concrete types - Create dependency requirement documentation - Task 2: Refactor EndorService internal implementation (AC: 2, 6) - Update all internal methods to use injected interface dependencies - Replace hard-coded repository access with injected RepositoryInterface - Replace direct configuration access with injected ConfigInterface - Maintain all existing method signatures and behaviors - Task 3: Implement backward compatibility layer (AC: 3) - Maintain NewEndorService() as convenience function with default dependencies - Create factory functions for common dependency configurations - Add migration guide documentation for existing users - Ensure existing test suite continues passing - Task 4: Integration with DI container (AC: 5) - Add container-based factory: NewEndorServiceFromContainer(container) - Register default EndorService factory in container configuration - Support dependency override patterns for testing - Add container validation for EndorService dependency requirements - Task 5: Comprehensive testing and validation (AC: 1-6) - Unit tests for constructor dependency injection patterns - Integration tests with DI container resolution - Mock-based testing demonstrating testability improvements - Performance validation ensuring no regression from interface indirection

story-context.acceptanceCriteria: 1. Constructor Dependency Injection - NewEndorServiceWithDeps() accepts all required dependencies as interface parameters with type safety 2. Interface-based Internal Dependencies - EndorService struct holds interface references (RepositoryInterface, ConfigInterface) instead of concrete types 3. Backward Compatibility - NewEndorService() convenience function maintains existing creation patterns with default implementations 4. Dependency Validation - Constructor validates required dependencies and provides clear error messages for missing dependencies 5. Container Integration - Service constructors work seamlessly with DI container for automatic dependency resolution 6. Method Compatibility - All existing EndorService methods work unchanged using injected interface dependencies

artifact.title: Product Requirements Document
artifact.section: FR1: Interface-based EndorService creation with dependency injection support
artifact.snippet: Framework must support constructor injection for EndorService with interface parameters, enabling custom implementations and comprehensive testing.

artifact.title: Architecture Document
artifact.snippet: Lightweight Custom DI Container with type-safe interface registration and resolution, supporting Singleton, Transient, and Scoped lifecycles.

artifact.title: Epic 2 Technical Specification
artifact.snippet: NewEndorServiceWithDeps() accepts Repository, Config, Logger, and Context interfaces as parameters with backward compatibility via convenience constructors.

artifact.title: Epic Breakdown
artifact.section: Story 2.2: Refactor EndorService for Constructor Injection
artifact.snippet: Enable constructor dependency injection for EndorService to support customization and testing while maintaining backward compatibility.

artifact.reason: Core service struct that needs dependency injection fields added
DI container interface for service resolution
Example of hard-coded GetMongoClient usage that needs injection
Framework initializer that will need DI container integration
Current singleton configuration pattern that needs interface abstraction
Current singleton MongoDB client that needs injection

framework_dependencies.interfaces: sdk/interfaces with extracted interfaces from Epic 1

constraints.architecture: Must follow lightweight dependency injection pattern without reflection overhead
constraints.compatibility: NewEndorService() convenience constructor must maintain existing creation patterns
constraints.performance: No regression in service creation or operation latency from interface indirection
constraints.validation: All required dependencies validated at construction time with clear error messages
constraints.testing: All dependencies must be mockable through interfaces for comprehensive unit testing

interface.name: ConfigProviderInterface
interface.signature: GetServerPort() string; GetDocumentDBUri() string; IsHybridResourcesEnabled() bool

interface.name: RepositoryInterface
interface.signature: Create(ctx, resource) error; Read(ctx, id, result) error; Update(ctx, resource) error; Delete(ctx, id) error

interface.name: EndorServiceInterface
interface.signature: GetResource() string; GetDescription() string; GetMethods() map[string]EndorServiceAction; Validate() error

interface.name: Container
interface.signature: Register[T](impl T, scope) error; Resolve[T]() (T, error); Validate() []error

tests.standards: All constructor injection functions require unit tests with mock dependencies; Interface compliance testing with compile-time checks; Integration tests with DI container resolution
tests.locations: sdk/interfaces_test.go for interface compliance; sdk/endor_service_test.go for constructor injection; sdk/testutils/ for mock implementations
tests.ideas: - AC1: Test NewEndorServiceWithDeps() with valid interfaces verifies type safety - AC2: Test EndorService internal methods use injected dependencies instead of globals - AC3: Test NewEndorService() convenience constructor provides working default dependencies - AC4: Test constructor validation with nil dependencies provides clear error messages - AC5: Test DI container can resolve EndorService with all dependencies automatically - AC6: Test existing EndorService method signatures and behaviors unchanged after refactoring
